approved_exception_note: No non-retrieval tooling edits are approved for this handoff; split tooling work into a separate lane.
kickoff_budget_note: Within the default AGENTS.md low-risk budget: 6 completed tasks, retrieval-owned paths only, no shared or integrator-locked edits, and required local gates rerun before handoff. The reviewed implementation commit set is explicit, and the metadata-only follow-up commit is not the feature payload.
roadmap_items: Milestone 4: Retrieval Layer -> FTS-first ingestion/index path for context/vault documents
scope_completed: The handoff now names the reviewed implementation commit set separately from the metadata-only follow-up commit, so the audit trail no longer implies that the follow-up commit is feature payload. The correction stays within the handoff artifacts only.
scope_goal: Document the retrieval handoff boundary while keeping the lane-owned retrieval scope constrained to the reviewed implementation commit set and excluding out-of-lane tooling edits.
tasks_completed: Added an explicit reviewed commit-set field so the implementation bundle is auditable.
Separated the metadata-only follow-up commit from the feature payload.
Kept the reviewed implementation files listed separately for traceability.
Kept the handoff correction within the lane-owned retrieval documentation artifacts.
compatibility_note: Breaking compatibility note: section: scopes are intentionally rejected in the owned retrieval service until section fallback support exists. Callers that depend on section targeting must switch to vault, collection:, or doc: scopes for the current FTS-first MVP path.
